# Figure description: 2015-2016 exploration index.jpg

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
This is an **exploration index map** (also functioning as a tenement location map) showing the spatial relationship between mineral exploration licence boundaries and geochemical sample location points in the Murchison region of Western Australia.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers the **Caddingwarra North** project area, located at approximately **117.75°E to 117.95°E longitude** and **27.25°S to 27.45°S latitude**. The primary tenement shown is **Exploration Licence E 2000742**, a large irregularly shaped tenure. Two smaller tenements are also depicted: **P 2002095** (a small prospecting licence with an irregular angular boundary) and **P 2002096** (a small prospecting licence immediately adjacent to the east). A cluster of geochemical sample points is concentrated in the central-western portion of the map, coinciding with the prospecting licences.

---

## Key Content
- **E 2000742** dominates the map as a large, staircase-shaped exploration licence with an irregular northern notch and a recessed southeastern corner.
- **P 2002095** is a small, angular-shaped prospecting licence located near the western-central area, with a distinctive irregular (possibly fault-following) boundary.
- **P 2002096** is a small rectangular-to-angular prospecting licence immediately east of P 2002095.
- A **dense cluster of geochemical sample points** (shown as a stippled/dotted pattern) is concentrated at the junction of P 2002095 and P 2002096, suggesting intensive surface sampling in this specific area.
- No geological units, lithological boundaries, or geophysical contours are displayed — this is purely a spatial index/location map.
- No colour coding is used; the map is monochrome (black and white).

---

---

## Research Value
This index map establishes the **spatial framework** for the Caddingwarra North exploration programme, confirming which tenements were held and where geochemical sampling was focused. The concentration of sample points at the P 2002095/P 2002096 boundary suggests a **specific geochemical target zone** was identified within the broader E 2000742 licence, likely guiding follow-up work. For a research assistant, this map answers the question of *where* exploration activity was concentrated and provides the tenement numbers needed to cross-reference drill hole data, geochemical results, and other technical reports in the WAMEX database.
